title: Roasted Ukrainian Grape Casserole
description: A comforting, velvety Ukrainian dish featuring roasted grapes, perfect for a sick day. This casserole is made with low-sodium ingredients and can be cooked in a clay pot.
instructions: Soak the grapes in cold water for 10 minutes, then drain and pat dry.
In a clay pot, combine the manteca, flour, and egg, mixing until smooth.
Add the grapes, sugar, vanilla extract, and salt-free seasoning blend to the pot, stirring until well combined.
Dot the top with butter and cover the pot with a lid.
Bake for 40 minutes, or until the grapes are tender and the casserole is golden brown.
Remove the lid and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned.
Let the casserole cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve warm, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Refrigerate leftovers for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
Thaw frozen casserole overnight in the refrigerator before reheating.
